assessor_raw: The cardiology startup has built a technically impressive AF detection model for ICU telemetry. The investor deck proposes repurposing this as a consumer wearable stroke-prevention screening tool. This requires three additional evidence types that are entirely absent: (1) signal-domain validation — the model must work on wrist-worn or patch device ECG/PPG, not just ICU electrode-quality telemetry; (2) screening epidemiology — at community AF prevalence (~1-4%), PPV will be substantially lower and false positive burden substantially higher than ICU-context performance implies; and (3) outcome pathway — the chain from wearable alert to stroke prevention involves care-seeking, diagnostic confirmation, anticoagulation prescribing, and adherence — none of these links are analyzed. The technology is promising and the ICU detection work is solid. The leap to stroke-prevention consumer screening is premature without a staged validation program. The deck should be restated as: 'ICU-validated AF detection technology; wearable validation and pathway evidence required before consumer screening deployment.'

empirical_test.measure: wearable device validation; PPV at community prevalence; alert-to-care pathway study
empirical_test.success_criterion: acceptable wearable performance; PPV >25% at community prevalence; >50% care-seeking from alerts
empirical_test.failure_criterion: significant performance degradation on wearable; PPV <15%; <30% care-seeking
